message:
feat(fsqlite-planner): implement compound SELECT ORDER BY resolution (bd-13r.3)

Add resolve_compound_order_by() matching C SQLite's exact behavior:
- Integer literal column refs (1-based)
- Alias resolution across all SELECTs with first-match priority
- COLLATE wrapper support, expression rejection
- 22 unit tests covering alias/numeric/error/conflict/case-insensitive cases
- 4 conformance fixtures (013-016) validated against C sqlite3 oracle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -587,6 +587,7 @@ dependencies = [
  "fsqlite-ast",
  "fsqlite-error",
  "fsqlite-types",
+ "tracing",
 ]
 
 [[package]]
@@ -597,6 +598,7 @@ dependencies = [
  "blake3",
  "proptest",
  "serde",
+ "serde_json",
  "smallvec",
  "xxhash-rust",
 ]

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+{
+  "id": "015_compound_order_by_error",
+  "description": "Compound SELECT ORDER BY error cases match C sqlite3",
+  "ops": [
+    { "op": "open", "path": ":memory:" },
+    {
+      "op": "exec",
+      "sql": "SELECT 1 AS a UNION SELECT 2 AS b ORDER BY z",
+      "expect_error": "ERROR"
+    },
+    {
+      "op": "exec",
+      "sql": "SELECT 1 AS a UNION SELECT 2 AS b ORDER BY a+0",
+      "expect_error": "ERROR"
+    }
+  ],
+  "fsqlite_modes": ["compatibility", "native"]
+}
